task_completion.description: Read the structural layout specification file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of structural_layout_spec.txt from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Open the initial eCRM-001 wing and tail model in OpenVSP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP application window is open and displays the eCRM-001_wing_tail.vsp3 model, showing both the main wing and tail components.

task_completion.description: Select the Main Wing component for modification
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP Geometry Browser shows the 'Main Wing' component highlighted or selected, with its parameter window open.

task_completion.description: Add and configure the front and rear spars
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Structure or Subsurface tab in the OpenVSP parameter window shows Spars being added, with parameters for the front and rear spar chord fractions visible.

task_completion.description: Add and configure the structural ribs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Structure or Subsurface tab in the OpenVSP parameter window shows Ribs being added, with the rib count or spacing parameter visible.

task_completion.description: Save the updated model with the new filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP 'Save As' dialog showing the file being saved as eCRM-001_structural.vsp3 in the Documents/OpenVSP directory, or the OpenVSP title bar updating to reflect the new filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenVSP GUI to configure the structures
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the OpenVSP graphical interface (Geometry Browser, parameter windows) rather than a text editor directly modifying the .vsp3 XML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not apply structural modifications to the tail components
integrity.visual_evidence: The tail components should not be selected in the Geometry Browser while the Structure tab is active, and no spars/ribs should be visible on the tails in the 3D view.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the modified model from OpenVSP
integrity.visual_evidence: The save operation should occur within the OpenVSP application (e.g., File -> Save As), rather than just copying the original file in the terminal without applying the structural changes.
